question: When simulating nanofluid heat transfer, the factors affecting thermal conductivity include ()
According to the time dependence of macroscopic properties in an ensemble, it can be divided into ________ ensembles and ________ ensembles, etc.

From a microscopic perspective, even if a system is in a definite macroscopic state, the motion states of each particle in the system may still be uncertain. The combination of the motion states of all particles in the system constitutes a ________.
In a system with fixed number of particles, volume, and temperature, the Helmholtz free energy contains all the ________ of the system.
In molecular dynamics simulations, when time approaches ________, this phase trajectory will traverse all phase points of the system.
________ refers to selecting an initial position coordinate for each particle in the system.
For any classical thermodynamic system at equilibrium, the velocities of all particles satisfy ________.
Choosing ________ boundary conditions usually allows the simulation system to be closer to the actual situation.
In classical molecular dynamics simulations, the interaction forces between particles are usually described by ________.
The potential energy of a classical many-particle system can be written as an analytic function of the ________ of each particle, no longer involving the solution of quantum mechanics problems.
To some extent, classical potentials can be divided into two major categories: ________ and ________.
Usually ________ is called a force field.
Relatively speaking, many-body potentials are ________ to the results of quantum mechanical calculations than two-body potentials.
The forces of many-body potentials satisfy the ________ of Newton's third law, but do not necessarily satisfy the ________ of Newton's third law.
Up to now there have been many empirical many-body potentials, among which the most widely used in the field of materials simulation are ________ and ________.
From the perspective of mathematical functions, the neural network model of machine learning potentials can be regarded as a ________.
In machine learning potentials, ________ is used to describe the atomic environment, i.e., the distribution of neighboring atoms.

The training of machine learning potentials is also called ________, the purpose is to optimize the trainable parameters in the machine learning potential according to the reference values of some physical quantities.
When atoms are very close to each other, there will be a strong ________ between them.
The main disadvantage of the Berendsen temperature control algorithm is that it does not correspond to ________.
Langevin dynamics was originally used to study the properties of ________ systems, and later was used to implement temperature control algorithms.
________ is the sum of the tensor product of particle position and force in the system.
Pressure is related to the coordinates of particles and the simulation box, so the pressure-control algorithm will ultimately reduce to operations on particle coordinates and ________.
Full degree-of-freedom pressure control independently controls ________ inequivalent pressure components.
When simulating two-dimensional materials, in the direction perpendicular to the plane where the material lies, ________ boundary conditions are often used.
The radial distribution function g(r) is proportional to the relative probability of finding pairs of atoms with separation near r in the system, and to a large extent reflects the system's ________.
Molecular dynamics simulation is based on classical mechanics and classical statistical mechanics, lacking ________.
In the thermodynamic integration method, although the result is expressed using a continuous integration form, in actual simulations we still need to return to the situation of ________.
Although transport phenomena occur in nonequilibrium states, according to ________, transport coefficients can still be calculated in equilibrium states.
Transport coefficients are related to the ________ of related physical quantities in equilibrium.
As long as the sampling interval is sufficiently small, the MSD of any system will have a parabolic ________ diffusion regime.
In molecular dynamics simulations, what is the main difference between ReaxFF and traditional fixed-bond force fields (such as CHARMM, AMBER)?
Why in NPT simulations can volume oscillations lead to energy fluctuations?
What is the truncation effect of the cutoff radius? How can it be mitigated?
In high-temperature or reactive systems, why is it necessary to reduce the time step?
Why is energy minimization generally performed before carrying out molecular dynamics simulations?
Why can't molecular dynamics simulations directly simulate phenomena on long time scales (such as millisecond-level diffusion)?
What role do the SHAKE and RATTLE algorithms play in molecular dynamics simulations?
What is velocity autocorrelation function (VACF)?
What is a coarse-grained force field, and what are its advantages and disadvantages?
Why is system equilibration crucial to simulation results in equilibrium molecular dynamics simulations, and how can one determine whether the system is equilibrated?
How to ensure the reproducibility and reliability of molecular dynamics simulation results?
How is the free energy change generally calculated in molecular dynamics simulations?
Why are enhanced sampling methods particularly important in long-time molecular dynamics simulation studies?
In molecular dynamics simulations, how are chemical reactions generally analyzed?
How to simulate a rarefied gas or vacuum environment in molecular dynamics simulations?
How to analyze interfacial wettability and determine the contact angle in molecular dynamics simulations?
How to analyze the hydrogen bond network in molecular dynamics simulations?
Why are rigid water models such as TIP3P and SPC/E commonly used when simulating water?
Why are the normal pressure and tangential pressure different when simulating a gas-liquid interface?
Why are many-body potentials (EAM/MEAM) commonly used in metal system simulations instead of the LJ potential (two-body potential)?
The difference between molecular dynamics and MonteCarlo simulation (hint: from theoretical methods, analysis methods, time-dependent motion, etc.)
Briefly describe how the energy of ab initio molecular dynamics (ab initio MD) is expressed? And give examples of related program names you know.
For a chemical reaction, how can the transition state of the reaction be obtained through simulation calculations; how should the program process be designed?

In molecular dynamics, in order to increase computational accuracy and shorten computation time, multiple computational measures need to be adopted. Please give examples and explain (discuss separately for the non-bonded interaction van der Waals potential and Coulomb interaction potential, such as periodic boundary conditions, nearest image, cutoff radius, and the Ewald summation method, etc.).
Briefly describe the basic principle of Monte Carlo simulation.

Estimate the average distance between air molecules under standard conditions, and compare this distance with the order of magnitude of the molecular diameter. (Given N_A = 6×10^23 mol^-1, and the molar volume of a gas under standard conditions is 22.4 L/mol.)

Analyze your data by several methods to obtain the melting temperature (such as mean square displacement, etc.). You should also propose other methods to obtain the melting temperature and compare their results. While recording the simulation parameters (such as time step, unit cell size, time to reach equilibrium, sampling time, ensemble, etc.), analyze whether this method overestimates or underestimates the melting point, and explain the reason.
For a face-centered cubic material, how many atoms are in a superlattice composed of N×N×N unit cells (unit cell)?

In each time step, if all atoms are considered, how many force calculations are needed? If a potential energy cutoff is considered, how many force calculations are needed? How many times do you need to calculate the distance between atoms?

For empirical potentials of simple systems, most of the computation time is spent calculating the distances between atoms. Can you find methods to reduce the time spent calculating interatomic distances?

options.A: Temperature reflects the intensity of thermal motion of each molecule
options.B: Temperature is the indicator of the average kinetic energy of molecules
options.C: For a certain mass of a certain substance, if internal energy increases, temperature will certainly rise
options.D: When temperature rises, the kinetic energy of each molecule of the object will increase

question: The reason that “a broken mirror is hard to make whole again” is

options.A: The repulsive force between glass molecules is greater than the attractive force
options.B: There is no molecular force acting between glass molecules
options.C: Within one piece of glass the attractive force between molecules is greater than the repulsive force; while between two pieces of broken glass, the molecular attractive force and repulsive force are equal in magnitude, and the resultant force is zero
options.D: Between two pieces of broken glass, the distances between the vast majority of glass molecules are too large, both molecular attractive and repulsive forces can be neglected, and the total molecular attractive force is zero

question: Among the following statements about molecular force and molecular potential energy, the correct one is

options.A: When the molecular force manifests as attraction, the molecular force and molecular potential energy always increase as the distance between molecules increases
options.B: When the molecular force manifests as attraction, the molecular force and molecular potential energy always decrease as the distance between molecules increases
options.C: When the molecular force manifests as repulsion, the molecular force and molecular potential energy always increase as the distance between molecules decreases
options.D: When the molecular force manifests as repulsion, the molecular force and molecular potential energy always decrease as the distance between molecules decreases

question: The number of liquid hydrogen molecules that can be contained in a cube with side length 1 nm is closest to which of the following values
If 6 billion people in the whole world simultaneously count the number of molecules in 1 g of water, each person counting 5000 per hour continuously, the required time is closest to
The temperature of an ice–water mixture is 0℃ and the masses are equal, then inside the glass cup

options.A: The average kinetic energy of ice molecules is greater than that of water molecules
options.B: The average kinetic energy of water molecules is equal to that of ice molecules
options.C: The kinetic energy of one water molecule must be greater than that of one ice molecule
options.D: The kinetic energy of one water molecule must be equal to that of one ice molecule

question: In the figure showing the relationship between the potential energy of two molecules and the intermolecular distance, the correct statement is

options.A: When the distance between two molecules r = r1, the molecular potential energy is zero, and the molecular force is also zero
options.B: When the distance between two molecules r = r1, the molecular potential energy is minimum, and the molecular force shows attraction
options.C: When the distance between two molecules r > r2, as r increases, the molecular force does negative work
options.D: When the distance between two molecules r > r2, as r increases, the molecular potential energy decreases

question: In a harmonic crystal system performing NVT simulation using a single Nosé–Hoover thermostat, if the thermostat relaxation time tau is set too small, the most likely phenomenon is

options.A: Temperature fluctuations are strictly suppressed to zero, and sampling still strictly follows the canonical ensemble
options.B: The dynamics become equivalent to white-noise Langevin dynamics
options.C: Resonance occurs with the system’s intrinsic vibrations, causing large periodic oscillations in the kinetic temperature and affecting sampling
options.D: It does not affect dynamics or sampling, only speeds up reaching equilibrium

question: When truncating the Lennard–Jones potential at r_c, which of the following statements about “potential shift”, “force shift”, and “switching function” is correct?

options.A: Potential shift makes the potential energy and force both continuous at r_c
options.B: Switching function can only guarantee the potential energy is continuous at r_c, the force must be discontinuous
options.C: Force shift can make the force at r_c continuous (and zero)
options.D: Simple truncation is equivalent to any smoothing treatment in the ensemble average of pressure

question: When calculating the self-diffusion coefficient D(L) in a liquid with three-dimensional periodic boundary conditions and a cubic box length L, considering the Yeh–Hummer finite-size correction, which of the following trend judgments is correct

options.A: D(L) is greater than the true value of the infinite system, and further increases as L increases
options.B: D(L) is less than the true value, and increases with L approaching the true value
options.D: D(L) is less than the true value, and decreases as L increases

question: When calculating the radial distribution function g(r) in a cubic periodic box, regarding the trustworthy r range, the correct statement is

options.A: It can be calculated all the way to half of the box diagonal L*sqrt(3)/2 without being affected
options.B: It should be limited to r < L/3, otherwise normalization fails
options.C: As long as the number of samples is large enough, r has no upper limit
options.D: Usually it is only trustworthy within r <= L/2; approaching L/2 the noise increases sharply due to geometric volume decay

question: In an MD simulation containing N atoms, with N_c complete constraints (such as SHAKE constraints) and with the total momentum of the system removed, the degrees of freedom DOF used to compute the kinetic temperature should be
For LJ liquid (reduced units T*=1, ρ*=0.85) using Velocity–Verlet NVE integration, which of the following time steps is most likely to maintain good energy conservation over a long time

options.D: Δt\* = 0.0005 but requires randomly resetting velocities

question: The core property of the Velocity–Verlet integrator in classical MD is

options.B: second-order accuracy, time-reversible and symplectic structure

question: In a three-dimensional cubic periodic box using the minimum image convention, the nonbonded interaction cutoff radius r_c should satisfy

options.A: r_c can be greater than the box length L
options.D: r_c is only related to density and is unrelated to the box length

question: Regarding the SHAKE and RATTLE constraint algorithms, the correct statement is

options.A: Both only constrain positions and do not affect velocities
options.B: Both only constrain velocities and do not affect positions
options.C: SHAKE applies constraints during the velocity update stage
options.D: RATTLE applies constraints in both the position and velocity stages

question: In an NVE simulation, when the total momentum drift of the system is not removed, the most likely consequence is

options.A: Does not affect any statistical quantity
options.B: MSD and diffusion coefficient are contaminated by the drift term and overestimated, need to be calculated using velocity relative to the center of mass
options.C: Only affects pressure, does not affect transport properties

question: For the Lennard–Jones potential using simple truncation and adding bulk long-range tail correction (LRC), the signs of the corrections to energy and pressure are usually
Which statement most accurately summarizes the significance of the Martyna–Tobias–Klein (MTTK) extension

options.A: Any thermostat + any pressure coupling all produce strict NPT
options.B: Parrinello–Rahman is self-consistent with any thermostat
options.C: The Berendsen method alone can strictly produce NPT fluctuations
options.D: MTTK together with Nosé–Hoover (chain) can strictly produce NPT sampling

question: When simulating a liquid film/interface using 3D PME while leaving a vacuum layer in the z direction, to avoid artifacts of interlayer dipole interactions, one should

options.A: Merely increasing the vacuum layer can completely eliminate them
options.B: Switch to real-space truncation to treat charges
options.C: Use the Yeh–Berkowitz slab correction or 2D Ewald/PME

question: In the Green–Kubo thermal conductivity formula, the quantity being integrated is

options.B: autocorrelation function of the microscopic heat flux vector

question: The long-time hydrodynamic tail decay behavior of the velocity autocorrelation function of a three-dimensional simple liquid is approximately
Regarding the stability of multi-time-step r-RESPA, which of the following is the most accurate

options.A: The larger the outer time step, the more stable it is
options.C: If the outer step size is too large, resonance instability will occur
options.D: As long as the energy drift is small it is definitely stable

question: The main characteristic of Parrinello–Rahman type anisotropic pressure control is

options.A: Only changes volume without changing shape
options.B: Allows box shape changes to sample stress-coupled lattice deformation

question: The most common Lorentz–Berthelot mixing rule corresponds to the interaction parameters
The core quantity for calculating viscosity by the Einstein–Helfand method is

options.A: the mean square displacement of velocity
options.B: the time integral of the stress autocorrelation function
options.C: the linear growth rate with time of the mean square of the Helfand moment
options.D: the position of the first peak of RDF

question: Which of the following statements about "configurational temperature" is correct

options.B: Depends on both position and velocity
options.D: Can be defined using configurational quantities such as force and its divergence, not dependent on velocity

question: The correct expression for calculating the coordination number N(r) from the radial distribution function g(r) is
The most appropriate virial expression for the pressure of a classical molecular system is

options.C: P = ρ k_B T − (1/3V) ⟨Σ r_ij · f_ij⟩ (the negative sign depends on the definition)

question: The most accurate description of the fundamental reason why symplectic integrators conserve energy well is

options.B: There exists a 'shadow Hamiltonian' close to the real Hamiltonian, and the energy oscillates slightly around it
options.C: The larger the time step, the smaller the energy drift
options.D: All symplectic integrators have zero error

question: The main purpose of applying bond length constraints in systems with a large number of X–H high-frequency vibrations (such as water) is

options.B: Allow larger time steps to reduce computational cost

question: The key property of the Bussi–Parrinello stochastic velocity rescaling thermostat (stochastic velocity rescaling) is

options.B: Ensures canonical sampling but strongly distorts dynamics
options.C: While ensuring canonical sampling, it perturbs the overall dynamics only slightly (global coupling)

question: The direct effect of increasing the damping parameter α in Ewald/PME is

options.A: Requires a larger real-space cutoff, reciprocal space cheaper
options.B: Real space shorter (faster decay), reciprocal space more expensive
options.C: No effect on the cost of the two parts
options.D: Total error is unrelated to α

question: The user accuracy control of PPPM/PME is usually set by which of the following indicators
In LJ reduced units, the correct form of the time unit τ is
In uniform shear NEMD, regarding temperature and thermostat treatment, the correct is

options.A: Directly thermostat the total velocity of particles
options.B: Should thermostat the “peculiar velocity” after removing the flow field
options.D: Only thermostat the velocity component in the flow direction

question: When using the "heating method" to estimate the melting point, to reduce systematic overestimation, one should prioritize

options.C: Introduce defects or free surfaces in the crystal to lower the nucleation barrier

question: Regarding common pressure controllers, the following statements are correct

options.A: Berendsen pressure coupling can strictly produce the correct NPT ensemble
options.B: Parrinello–Rahman/MTTK type methods allow anisotropic cell fluctuations and under reasonable parameters produce correct NPT sampling
options.C: Berendsen pressure coupling is suitable for formal output of thermodynamic fluctuation properties
options.D: Berendsen pressure coupling will suppress volume fluctuations, thereby underestimating the isothermal compressibility

question: When using the "heating-to-melt" method to estimate the melting point of a solid, which of the following strategies are effective to reduce superheating bias

options.A: Pre-place a small number of defects in the crystal or introduce a free surface
options.B: Significantly increase the heating rate to cross the barrier as soon as possible
options.D: Use the solid–liquid two-phase coexistence method under constant pressure to determine the temperature at which the interface is stationary

question: When using the Green–Kubo formula to calculate viscosity, which of the following practices or judgments are correct

options.A: It is necessary to perform time integration of the shear stress autocorrelation function (usually take three off-diagonal components and average)
options.B: Only integrating the velocity autocorrelation function is sufficient to obtain viscosity
options.C: The long-time tail contributes significantly to the integral, and insufficient sampling time often leads to underestimation of viscosity
options.D: Strongly coupled Langevin thermostat will change stress fluctuations and is not suitable for formal GK viscosity calculation

question: Regarding the long-range correction (LRC) of the Lennard–Jones potential, which of the following statements are correct

options.A: LRC assumes the fluid beyond r_c is uniform and isotropic, therefore it is more suitable for bulk liquids
options.B: In the presence of a liquid–gas interface or strongly non-uniform systems, LRC still strictly holds
options.C: Adding LRC can reduce the systematic deviation of energy and pressure with respect to the cutoff radius
options.D: LRC will eliminate the force discontinuity problem at r_c

question: Regarding the influence of thermostats on dynamics and transport properties, which statements are appropriate

options.A: Weakly coupled Nosé–Hoover chains relatively distort long-time-scale dynamics less and have a smaller impact on transport properties
options.B: A common practice is to first equilibrate with NVT, then switch to NVE to calculate transport coefficients
options.C: The Berendsen thermostat can strictly reproduce the canonical ensemble fluctuations in experiments
options.D: Stochastic thermostats will never change the self-diffusion coefficient

question: Regarding the Ewald/PME treatment of charged systems, which statements are correct

options.A: The computational cost of traditional Ewald grows approximately as O(N^1.5–N^2) with the number of particles, while PME/PPPM is about O(N log N)
options.B: In PME, using a finer FFT grid or higher-order interpolation will improve the accuracy of the reciprocal-space part but increase the cost
options.C: Under a fixed total error, increasing the real-space cutoff can usually use a coarser reciprocal-space grid (the error can be balanced between the two parts)
options.D: Under three-dimensional periodic boundaries the total charge must be zero; if not zero, the Ewald/PME summation diverges (some implementations implicitly add a uniform neutralizing background)

question: Which statements about the Nosé–Hoover chain (NHC) are correct

options.A: Moderately increasing the chain length (e.g., 3–5) helps improve coverage for multimodal systems
options.B: When the chain length is 1, ergodicity is always guaranteed
options.C: An excessively small thermostat time constant may resonate with intrinsic modes

question: Regarding the choice of pressure controllers, which statements are correct

options.A: Berendsen pressure coupling can give correct volume fluctuations
options.B: The MTTK scheme can produce strict NPT under reasonable parameters
options.C: An isotropic barostat is not suitable for simulating crystal deformation with anisotropic stress present
options.D: Too short pressure coupling time will introduce strong oscillations of box shape/volume and sampling bias

question: Regarding Ewald/PME/PPPM, which statements hold

options.A: The nominal complexity of PME/PPPM is approximately O(N log N)
options.B: Grid density, interpolation order, and damping parameter jointly determine accuracy and cost
options.C: The splitting parameter changes the allocation of the exact sum, but under a given error target it can balance between real/reciprocal space
options.D: Under 3D periodicity the total charge must be zero, often achieved by neutralizing with a uniform background

question: Regarding the long-range correction (LRC) of the LJ potential, which statements are correct

options.A: Assumes the region beyond the cutoff is a uniform isotropic bulk phase, therefore more suitable for bulk liquids
options.B: Still strictly valid in strongly inhomogeneous systems such as liquid–gas interfaces
options.C: Adding LRC can reduce the systematic bias of energy and pressure with r_c
options.D: LRC can fix the discontinuity of the force at r_c

question: Regarding common constraint implementations, which statements are correct

options.A: LINCS belongs to the matrix projection method, requires fewer iterations, and is more stable for high constraint degree
options.B: SHAKE applies positional constraints by iteratively correcting distances
options.C: Introducing constraints can remove high-frequency modes, thereby allowing a larger time step
options.D: When calculating pressure, the contribution of constraints to the virial can be ignored

question: Comparison of free energy calculation methods, which statements are correct

options.A: BAR has approximately minimal variance when there is overlap between the two states
options.B: MBAR extends BAR to multiple states and is a maximum likelihood estimation framework
options.C: The variance of thermodynamic integration (TI) is always smaller than BAR
options.D: Lack of overlap between states will cause BAR/MBAR variance to increase significantly

question: Regarding (well-tempered) metadynamics, which statements are correct

options.A: well-tempered suppresses overfilling by decreasing deposition strength over time
options.B: If the collective variables omit slow degrees of freedom it will lead to convergence difficulties
options.C: The long-term bias approximately converges to -F(CV) (up to a constant)
options.D: Under metadynamics it is impossible to perform reweighting estimation for arbitrary quantities

question: Regarding Umbrella Sampling/WHAM statements, which are correct

options.A: Adjacent windows need sufficient overlap to ensure WHAM/MBAR stability
options.B: A harmonic bias is commonly used, post-processing needs to remove the bias and perform normalization
options.C: Even with completely no overlap between windows the PMF can be reconstructed without bias
options.D: Windowing reduces the first arrival time of crossing the barrier

question: Regarding the calculation of viscosity and thermal conductivity, which statements are correct

options.A: Viscosity can be obtained from the GK integral of stress autocorrelation or the slope of the Helfand moment
options.B: Both are affected by long-time tails and statistical noise, requiring long sampling
options.C: Strong thermostat/strong damping has no effect on fluctuations
options.D: NEMD (periodic perturbation, SLLOD, etc.) can serve as independent cross-validation

question: Effects of finite size on the self-diffusion coefficient, which statements are correct

options.A: Under 3D PBC, the deviation of D(L) relative to the infinite system ~ 1/L
options.B: Switching to NVE can eliminate this finite-size effect
options.C: The magnitude of the correction term is inversely proportional to the fluid viscosity η (k_B T/(6π η L))
options.D: The deviation of D(L) is independent of the charge model and is always 1/L^3

question: Regarding the implementation of heat flux and GK thermal conductivity under many-body potentials, which statements are valid

options.A: The heat flux of many-body potentials needs to include a consistent partitioning of the many-body terms
options.B: The partitioning based on atomic energy is not unique, and different definitions may introduce implementation differences
options.C: In the bulk limit, the macroscopic thermal conductivity under reasonable definitions should be consistent
options.D: Using only the kinetic energy part can obtain the correct thermal conductivity

question: Regarding practical experience of the r-RESPA multiple time step method, which are correct

options.A: Fast-varying bond/angle interactions are updated every step, slowly varying non-bonded interactions are updated at low frequency
options.B: Setting the outer timestep too large will induce resonance instability
options.D: Long-range charge interactions can be placed in the outer step to reduce cost, but need careful validation

question: Regarding rigid bodies and quaternion integration, which statements are correct

options.A: Quaternion representation avoids the Euler angle gimbal lock problem
options.B: In numerical implementation it is necessary to periodically normalize quaternions to suppress drift
options.C: Using constraint methods can also treat water molecules as rigid bodies
options.D: When calculating temperature the contribution of rotational degrees of freedom should be included

question: Regarding force field mixing rules and tail corrections, which statements are correct

options.A: Lorentz–Berthelot: σ_ij takes the arithmetic mean, ε_ij takes the geometric mean
options.B: Waldman–Hagler and other rules can replace LB
options.C: Mixing rules only affect the interaction parameters of cross-species pairs
options.D: The magnitude of the tail correction is unrelated to the interaction parameters

question: Regarding the judgment of "reaching equilibrium", which are more reasonable

options.A: If potential energy and density are stable, equilibrium can be asserted
options.B: The distributions are stable over time and the correlation times of key correlation functions are finite
options.C: Use block averaging/autocorrelation to estimate statistical inefficiency to evaluate errors
options.D: Only need to extend the simulation time to automatically eliminate systematic bias

question: Which statements about reproducibility are correct

options.A: Deterministic integration on different parallel/hardware may lead to exponential divergence of trajectories due to differences in rounding and reduction order
options.B: Statistical ensemble quantities should be consistent under sufficient sampling
options.C: Fixing the random seed can guarantee bitwise consistency across platforms
options.D: Reproducing experiments should record all key inputs, parameters, and random seeds

question: After diluting ink with water and observing under a microscope, which of the following statements are correct

options.A: Under the microscope one can see both water molecules and suspended small carbon particles
options.B: The small carbon particles are constantly making irregular motion, this is the so-called Brownian motion
options.C: The smaller the carbon particles, the more obvious the motion
options.D: The liquid that appears connected into one piece under the microscope are actually stationary water molecules

question: In the oleic acid oil film method experiment, given the oil film area S, molar mass M, density ρ and NA, the following expressions are correct

options.A: The diameter of an oleic acid molecule d=...
options.B: The diameter of an oleic acid molecule d=...
options.C: The number of molecules contained in the oleic acid n=NA
options.D: The number of molecules contained in the oleic acid n=NA

question: In the graph of intermolecular force and intermolecular potential energy between two molecules as a function of distance, the following statements are correct:

options.A: When the intermolecular distance is r0, the intermolecular force and intermolecular potential energy are both minimal and zero
options.B: When the intermolecular distance r>r0, the intermolecular force increases as the intermolecular distance increases
options.C: When the intermolecular distance r>r0, the intermolecular potential energy increases as the intermolecular distance increases

question: For a system containing N atoms, with N_c complete constraints and with the total momentum of the system removed, the degrees of freedom used to compute the kinetic temperature should be __________.
Under 3D cubic box PBC, the Yeh–Hummer correction for the self-diffusion coefficient is D(∞) = D(L) + ξ·k_B T/(6π η L), where the constant ξ = __________.
In the Green–Kubo viscosity formula, the correlation function that is integrated over time is the autocorrelation function of __________.

Under three-dimensional periodic boundaries, when using Ewald/PME summation, the electroneutrality condition that must be satisfied is that the __________ of the system is zero (otherwise a uniform neutralizing background is usually implicitly added).
The long-range correction (LRC) of the Lennard–Jones potential assumes that the fluid beyond the cutoff radius r_c is __________.
To reduce the resonance risk between a single Nosé–Hoover thermostat and the intrinsic modes of the system, it is often extended to __________.
To allow anisotropic unit cell fluctuations and obtain correct NPT sampling, the commonly used pressure controller is __________.
In a cubic periodic box, the maximum reliable radius for calculating the radial distribution function g(r) is usually limited to __________.
The definition of LJ reduced temperature is T\* = __________.
The reduced time unit τ of the LJ system is defined as τ = __________.

When using the solid–liquid two-phase coexistence method to determine the melting point, the criterion for judging that the equilibrium melting point has been reached is that the average drift velocity of __________ is zero.

In the canonical ensemble, the relative fluctuation of temperature for a constrained system satisfies the approximate relation Var(T)/T^2 ≈ __________, where the denominator is the effective degrees of freedom.
Uniform shear nonequilibrium molecular dynamics (NEMD) commonly uses the __________ algorithm together with Lees–Edwards boundary conditions.
The commonly used basis function for interpolating charges onto the grid in PME is __________.
A general method to introduce quantum nuclear effects in classical MD is __________.
